subject: Human Resources
title: Hybrid Work Models
description: A short lesson to help you apply Hybrid Work Models.

hook.text: Employees want flexibility. Executives want collaboration. Hybrid work promises both—but delivers neither when done badly.

buildup.text: Hybrid models combine remote and in-office work—typically 2-3 office days per week, with the rest flexible.

discovery.text: Successful hybrid requires intentional design—coordinated in-office days for collaboration, remote days for deep focus work.

twist.text: The worst hybrid: everyone's in the office on different days, so they're still on Zoom—commuting just to sit in the same meetings.

climax.text: Hybrid works when the office has a purpose—collaboration, mentoring, culture-building—not just occupying a desk with a monitor.

punchline.text: Hybrid isn't half remote, half office. It's intentional about when and why each happens.

quiz.question: What makes hybrid work fail?
options: When office days aren't coordinated so people still use Zoom from their desks
When employees have too much flexibility
When the office is too nice
